quick_facts.label: Award and focus
quick_facts.value: B.Tech in Dairy Technology is an undergraduate professional degree in dairy science and engineering. At ICAR-NDRI, it is described as intensive training in milk processing, quality control of milk products, and engineering aspects of dairy-processing equipment.

quick_facts.label: Typical length
quick_facts.value: The programme is normally four academic years (eight semesters). ICAR-NDRI also specifies a maximum completion period of seven years for its B.Tech (Dairy Technology) programme.

quick_facts.label: School-level preparation
quick_facts.value: Requirements differ by admitting institution. For the ICAR 2025 UG route, candidates needed 10+2 with at least three core subjects from Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology, and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture; institutions can impose more specific combinations such as PCM, PCB, PCMB, or English requirements.

quick_facts.label: National admission route
quick_facts.value: For the 2025–26 cycle, ICAR-linked undergraduate seats in B.Tech Dairy Technology were allotted using merit in CUET (ICAR-UG) and online counselling. Admission rules, seat matrices, subject combinations, and dates are annual and must be checked in the applicable year’s official bulletin.

quick_facts.label: Practice-based learning
quick_facts.value: At ICAR-NDRI, the programme includes compulsory six-month internship/in-plant training in the seventh and eighth semesters. Its published course list also includes rural dairy work experience and an experiential-learning module.

sections.heading: Eligibility: check the university’s exact subject rule
paragraphs: B.Tech Dairy Technology eligibility is not completely uniform across India. The ICAR CUET (ICAR-UG) 2025 bulletin required applicants to have passed Class 12 or an equivalent examination after 12 years of study, with at least three core subjects among Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Biology and Agriculture/Inter-Agriculture. It also set aggregate-mark thresholds of 50% for General, OBC (NCL), UPS and EWS categories and 40% for SC, ST, Third Gender and PwBD categories for the ICAR examination route.

An applicant must then meet the individual university’s programme rule. For example, ICAR-NDRI’s 2025–26 bulletin accepted Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics or Physics, Chemistry, Biology, plus English, and specified 50% aggregate marks, with 40% for SC/ST and PH candidates. The ICAR’s university-wise list shows that other Dairy Technology institutions used differing combinations and conditions. Candidates should therefore treat the current year’s university prospectus and ICAR/NTA bulletin—not a generic eligibility summary—as controlling.
bullets: A Class 12 science background is essential; confirm whether the target institution accepts PCM, PCB, PCMB, Agriculture, and/or requires English.
Confirm institution-specific age, domicile, medical/physical, category-certificate, and document-verification conditions before registering.

Do not assume that qualifying for CUET automatically establishes eligibility for every preferred university; the ICAR bulletin makes fulfilment of the admitting institution’s requirements the candidate’s responsibility.

sections.heading: Duration and training structure
paragraphs: The standard B.Tech Dairy Technology structure is four years across eight semesters. ICAR-NDRI states that its academic year contains two semesters and that the B.Tech programme must ordinarily be completed within a maximum of seven years.

The degree is designed to combine classroom, laboratory and plant exposure. ICAR-NDRI’s 2025–26 bulletin makes a six-month internship compulsory and places in-plant training in the seventh and eighth semesters. At LUVAS, the published regulations similarly provide for industry work experience in a dairy factory or industry, rural dairy work experience, and experiential learning.
bullets: Expect a semester-based programme rather than a short vocational course.
Plan for substantial practical, laboratory, field and dairy-plant work, including a final-stage industry/in-plant component.
Exact internship timing, credit value and completion rules are institution-specific.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: The curriculum sits at the intersection of dairy-product technology, food science, engineering, microbiology/chemistry, quality systems and dairy business management. ICAR’s minimum standards for Dairy Technology identify the core academic areas as Dairy Technology, Dairy Engineering, Dairy Chemistry, Dairy Microbiology, Dairy Trade and Dairy Business Management, supported by a pilot dairy/business-process-development facility.

A representative current course list published by ICAR-NDRI includes market milk; traditional Indian dairy products; fat-rich products; cheese; ice cream and frozen desserts; dairy by-products; packaging; condensed and dried milks; sensory evaluation; dairy-plant management; waste disposal and pollution abatement; rural dairy work experience; experiential learning; and in-plant training. LUVAS’s published curriculum also evidences engineering content such as fluid mechanics, thermodynamics, heat and mass transfer, refrigeration, dairy process engineering, instrumentation, process control and plant design/layout.

This means the degree is not limited to making dairy products: students work with processing operations, equipment, hygiene and quality control, packaging, product evaluation, resource efficiency, waste management and commercial/management considerations.
bullets: Dairy processing and product technology: fluid milk, traditional products, cheese, frozen products, dried and condensed products, and by-products.
Engineering and operations: heat transfer, refrigeration, processing equipment, automation/process control, energy and plant layout.
Quality and commercial capability: microbiology and chemistry foundations, sensory evaluation, packaging, food-safety regulation, marketing and entrepreneurship/management modules.

sections.heading: Admission through ICAR-linked counselling and university routes
paragraphs: For the ICAR agricultural-university system, the documented 2025–26 route was CUET (ICAR-UG), conducted by the National Testing Agency, followed by ICAR online counselling and seat allotment based on merit rank, preferences and eligibility. ICAR-NDRI specifically stated that candidates seeking its B.Tech programmes had to opt for the institute during the CUET counselling process.

B.Tech Dairy Technology is listed among the four-year professional bachelor programmes available through CUET (ICAR-UG) in the 2025 bulletin. The same bulletin lists participating universities and their Dairy Technology seat information and conditions; the final available seat count was stated to be displayed at the time of online counselling. This makes annual verification important: participating institutions, seats, test-subject requirements, application windows and counselling schedules may change.

The node’s parent label “ICAR AIEEA” reflects the older ICAR entrance-examination naming commonly used in education discussions. For the cited 2025–26 undergraduate cycle, the official route was labelled CUET (ICAR-UG), while ICAR continued to use AIEEA (PG) for postgraduate admissions.
bullets: Read the NTA CUET-UG and ICAR UG information bulletins for the target admission year before selecting test subjects.
During counselling, rank eligible Dairy Technology institutions only after checking each institution’s Class 12 subject combination, marks, age and other special conditions.

Keep the confirmation page, payment proof, score/rank documents, Class 10/12 records, category/EWS/PwBD certificates where applicable, and other documents specified for counselling and university verification.

sections.heading: After graduation: industry, entrepreneurship and further study
paragraphs: Graduates can pursue technical and quality-oriented work in dairy and food processing, including production/plant operations, product processing, quality-control or quality-assurance laboratory work, packaging, and dairy-plant management. These are capability-aligned pathways rather than guaranteed job titles: ICAR-NDRI describes the degree as training in processing, product quality control and dairy-processing equipment, and its placement cell describes a four-year programme with practical coursework and six months of dairy-plant training. An ICAR-NDRI recruitment notice also listed B.Tech Dairy Technology among acceptable qualifications for young-professional work in milk-quality and safety laboratory sections.

Further study is a principal route. ICAR-NDRI offers postgraduate Dairy Technology and related Dairying disciplines, and its published doctoral eligibility includes a four-year B.Tech degree in Dairy Technology/Dairy Engineering or specified related fields for Ph.D. Dairy Technology. ICAR’s AIEEA (PG) is the national examination route associated with postgraduate agricultural education; applicants should use the current NTA/ICAR notice and the target programme’s discipline eligibility rather than relying on an older syllabus or bulletin.

Management and entrepreneurship are additional directions. The undergraduate curriculum can include entrepreneurship, business management and industrial consultancy, while a CAT-based MBA route is academically possible because MBA admissions commonly require a recognised bachelor’s degree; individual business schools impose their own score, marks, work-experience and selection requirements. GATE eligibility and accepted paper/discipline requirements also need to be checked against the current official GATE information brochure and the receiving institution’s M.Tech rules.
bullets: Technical careers: dairy technologist, production/plant operations roles, graduate engineer trainee roles, quality-control/quality-assurance analyst roles, product-development support, packaging and dairy-equipment/process roles.

Higher education: M.Tech/M.Sc./Master in Dairying pathways in Dairy Technology, Dairy Engineering, Dairy Chemistry, Dairy Microbiology, Food Technology or food-safety-related areas, subject to each programme’s eligibility.

Examinations and transitions: ICAR AIEEA (PG) for relevant postgraduate agricultural programmes; CAT for MBA pathways; GATE where the current examination and receiving institute accept the degree/paper combination.

Entrepreneurship: dairy processing, value-added dairy products, quality/testing services, cold-chain-linked operations or food-processing ventures require separate regulatory, finance, food-safety and market planning.
